pr.title: PR #147 — Evidence to Promotion Loop v1
pr.description:
## Summary M2 — proves that execution evidence **changes capability state** instead of remaining an inert artifact (closes the EVID-001 orphaning gap from PR #144). A thin `EvidencePromotionLoop` reads the `evidence.json` bundle that `execution-chain-run` already produces, derives a pass/fail outcome, and accumulates it onto the capability's existing confidence/readiness via the existing `CapabilityConfidenceEngine`. **No new promotion framework, no doctrine, no new registry** — the confidence record (keyed by `capability_id`) *is* the durable state. ### Evidence source used The `execution-chain-run` output: `artifacts/execution_chain//evidence.json` (carries `references.capability_id/result_id/artifact_id`) + sibling `trace.json` (carries `status` PASS/FAIL, `report_id`, `created_at`). No orchestrator change was needed. ### Existing structures reused (no gap → nothing new invented) | M2 need | Existing structure reused | |---|---| | capability identity | `capability_id` (chain evidence `references.capability_id`; the key `CapabilityConfidenceEngine` already uses) | | evidence + pass/fail | chain `evidence.json` bundle + `trace.json` `status` | | confidence / readiness / state | `CapabilityConfidenceEngine` (score 0–1, level very_low…very_high); readiness derived from score | | recency | existing `created_at` ISO timestamps (opt-in `--max-age-seconds`) | | rejected/quarantined | thin intake audit record (see below) | The one thin addition: rejected/quarantined evidence produces **no** confidence report (no state change), so the confidence store cannot record the rejection itself. A small per-application intake record (`artifacts/capability_evidence_intake//report.json` + `pass_fail.json`, following the repo's standard per-engine evidence convention) captures decision + reason + before/after + links. It is an audit artifact, **not** a new promotion registry. ### Evidence → state mapping (pseudocode) ``` outcome = bundle.passed | bundle.status | trace.status -> PASS | FAIL | MISSING identity = override | bundle.capability_id | references.capability_id if not identity: decision = QUARANTINED (no state change) elif file missing/unreadable: decision = REJECTED (MISSING) elif stale(created_at, max_age_seconds): decision = QUARANTINED elif outcome == MISSING: decision = REJECTED (no state change) else: # ACCEPTED prior = latest confidence report for capability_id (factors) or zeros execution_count += 1; success_count += (outcome==PASS); failure_count += (outcome==FAIL) report = CapabilityConfidenceEngine.create(capability_id, **cumulative_factors) readiness = ready (score>=0.7) | provisional (>=0.3) | blocked ``` ### Before/after state example (real run) `execution-chain-run self-model-build` (PASS) → apply: - confidence `very_low (0.0)` → `very_high (1.0)`; readiness `blocked` → `ready` - linked to `capability_id`, `result_id`, `artifact_id`, `report_id` of that run ### Accepted vs rejected/quarantined behavior - **Accepted**: PASS raises confidence; FAIL accumulates a failure (can drop readiness to `blocked`). - **Quarantined**: missing capability identity; stale evidence (older than `--max-age-seconds`). No state change. - **Rejected**: missing evidence file or no determinable outcome. No state change. ### CLI - `axiom capability-evidence-apply --evidence [--capability-id ] [--max-age-seconds ] [--artifacts-root

] [--json-output]` — shows capability, evidence path/id, outcome, decision (accepted/rejected/quarantined), reason, prior→updated confidence/readiness, and links. - `axiom capability-evidence-history [--capability-id ]` — queryable state-change log. - `axiom capability-evidence-show ` — one record. ### Tests and validation numbers New `tests/test_evidence_promotion.py` (passing, failing, missing-outcome, missing-file, no-identity → quarantine, override, repeated/cumulative, stale → quarantine, fresh, determinism, queryable, M3 hook, real-chain integration, 3 CLI). Registry expectations updated. - targeted (`test_evidence_promotion` + `test_command_registry` + `test_execution_chain_orchestrator`): **61 passed** - full `pytest`: **5045 passed, 1 skipped** - `ruff`: clean ### M3 hook (recorded only) Each record carries an `m3_hook` listing semantic info that would improve evidence routing (capability purpose, layer, intended use, consumers, dependencies, evidence expectations). Purpose/Layer Index is **not** implemented here. ### Non-goals confirmed No new promotion doctrine/architecture, no new framework family, no M3 index, no M5 work, no Execution Graph Synthesizer, no Organizational State schema, no canonical file migration, no broad refactor, no Revit-runtime validation. ### Unresolved questions (routed, not designed) - **Program 6**: promotion doctrine — when does accumulated confidence/readiness justify a `global_capability_registry` status transition (PROPOSED→OPEN→MERGED)? Not decided here. - **Program 2**: should evidence weighting account for which upstream capabilities/dependencies were exercised (needs M3 semantics)? - **Program 7**: a recency/decay policy beyond binary stale-quarantine (half-life on confidence factors)? Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/8ad1c9ac0d2f4252bb77647f1eabaac7 Requested by: @plamen-dev
pr.author: plamen-dev
